---
title: "Bayes Regression - Example"
format: html
---

Algorithm for Union Falls Pond:

Questions:
- Line 53: how do you know what values are best?

```{r}
# step 0 - load in data and packages

library(tidyverse)

# step 1 - write out the vb function, grab from vb quarto

vb_function <- function(t, max_l, k, b) {
  max_l * (1 - exp(-k * (t - b)))}

# step 2 - compute prior for max_l

compute_prior_log <- function(max_l, k, b) {
  dnorm(max_l, mean = 0, sd = 1000, log = TRUE)
}

# step 3 - set k, b, sigma^2 as constants

k <-  0.3183 
b <-  -0.8749 
sigma2 <- 30 

# step 4 - compute log likelihood (make function)

compute_likelihood_log <- function(max_l) {
  dnorm(predict_unionfallspond$Length, 
        mean =  von_bert_function(clams_unionfallspond$growth_period, max_l, k, b),
        sd = sqrt(sigma2), log = TRUE) |>
    sum() # call function
  
}

# step 5 - sample call 

compute_likelihood_log(max_l = 30)
compute_likelihood_log(max_l = 40)
compute_likelihood_log(max_l = 50)


# step 6 - compute posterior log

compute_posterior_log <- function(max_l) {
  compute_prior_log(max_l) + compute_likelihood_log(max_l)
}

# step 7 - sample call

compute_posterior_log(max_l = 30)
compute_posterior_log(max_l = 40)
compute_posterior_log(max_l = 50)


## define a proposal/jumping distribution to be N(0, 0.1)


## set-up the chain
niter <- 10000

# set up three empty vectors, one for each parameter
max_l_store <- rep(NA, 10000) 

# define a starting value
max_l_store[1] <- 1.2 



for (i in 2:niter) {
 
 max_l_current <- max_l_store[i - 1]

  ## propose a new value with proposal/jumping distribution
  ## here, my proposal/jumping distribution is N(current_beta1, 0.1)
  ## this distribution must be symmetric around the current beta1 (or else would need adjustment)
  proposal_sd <- 1
  ## proposal_sd can't be too large or too small, as discussed in the reading.

  max_l_proposal <- rnorm(1, mean = max_l_current, sd = proposal_sd)

  ## compute acceptance probability - do this in machine learning i think
  alpha_l <- min(1, exp(compute_posterior_log(max_l_proposal) - compute_posterior_log(max_l_current)))
  

  ## maybe update beta1 with acceptance probability alpha
  max_l_store[i] <- sample(c(max_l_proposal, max_l_current), size = 1,
                           prob = c(alpha_l, 1 - alpha_l))
}

## plots
## filter removes "start-up"
plot_df <- tibble(iter = 1:niter, max_l = max_l_store, k = k_store, b = b_store) |>
  filter(iter > 2000)

## make sure that this seems "stable" (isn't trailing off in an increasing
## or decreasing direction)
ggplot(data = plot_df, aes(x = iter, y = max_l)) +
  geom_line()

plot_df |>
  summarise(mean_l = mean(max_l),
            sd = sd(max_l))
## check these with estimator for beta1 and its SE from lm: they should be close!

## shape of posterior
ggplot(data = plot_df, aes(x = max_l)) +
  geom_histogram(colour = "skyblue4", fill = "skyblue1", bins = 15)

```
